This check is a good addition to handle incomplete API data, but it only covers the `temp` variable. Other variables like `feels`, `desc`, and `humidity` can also be `null` or empty, which would lead to undesirable output in the tooltip (e.g., `Feels like nullآ°C`).

To make the script more robust, I suggest extending the check to all variables used in the output.

Additionally, since this script is executed by bash, using the `[[ ... ]]` conditional construct is preferred over `[ ... ]` as it is more robust and powerful. Here is a suggestion that addresses both points:

```
        if [[ "$temp" == "null" || -z "$temp" || "$feels" == "null" || -z "$feels" || "$desc" == "null" || -z "$desc" || "$humidity" == "null" || -z "$humidity" ]]; then
          echo '{"text": "َ°–گ --", "tooltip": "Weather data incomplete"}'
          exit 0
        fi
```","created_at":"2026-03-18T22:51:12Z","updated_at":"2026-03-18T22:51:12Z","html_url":"https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/361#discussion_r2956665228","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/361","_links":{"self":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/2956665228"},"html":{"href":"https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/361#discussion_r2956665228"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/361"}},"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/2956665228/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"start_line":20,"original_start_line":20,"start_side":"RIGHT","line":23,"original_line":23,"side":"RIGHT","author_association":"NONE","original_position":8,"position":8,"subject_type":"line"}]

content.text:
All CI checks are passing and there are no merge conflicts. There's one review comment from gemini-code-assist suggesting we extend the null check to cover `feels`, `desc`, and `humidity` variables too, and use `[[ ]]` instead of `[ ]`.

Let me address this review comment.
The review comment is reasonable â€” extend the null check to all variables and use `[[ ]]`. Let me apply the fix.

input.old_string:
# Bail out if API returned incomplete data
        if [ "$temp" = "null" ] || [ -z "$temp" ]; then
          echo '{"text": "َ°–گ --", "tooltip": "Weather data incomplete"}'
          exit 0
        fi
input.new_string:
# Bail out if API returned incomplete data
        if [[ "$temp" == "null" || -z "$temp" || "$feels" == "null" || -z "$feels" || "$desc" == "null" || -z "$desc" || "$humidity" == "null" || -z "$humidity" ]]; then
          echo '{"text": "َ°–گ --", "tooltip": "Weather data incomplete"}'
          exit 0
        fi
